seasonal_wine.name: Furmint February, Budapest dry wine season
seasonal_wine.description: Each January and February, Budapest wine bars, restaurants and the Tokaj region itself run the Furmint February campaign promoting dry Furmint as a internationally recognised dry white wine. Dozens of venues across Budapest offer by-the-glass and flight menus of dry Furmint from the region's leading estates. It is a key seasonal promotional window for Tokaj's dry wine identity and attracts international press and buyers visiting Hungary.
seasonal_wine.tip: Most Budapest restaurants participate; arrive early in February to catch the widest selection before popular single-vineyard bottlings sell through.

seasonal_wine.name: Spring primary tasting season, March-April
seasonal_wine.description: March and April mark the opening of the primary tasting season at Tokaj estates. After the winter bottling period, the new vintage of dry Furmint and Szamorodni száraz (dry) is typically released, and cellar doors reopen for appointments. The spring season coincides with the Buda and Pest restaurant wine list updates, when Budapest sommeliers travel to the Hegyalja to taste new releases. The weather is mild and the hills are in early bud-break, making it ideal for vineyard walks.
seasonal_wine.tip: Call ahead to confirm cellar openings, especially at small estates like Bott Pince and Pendits; larger producers like Royal Tokaji and Disznókő accept walk-in appointments from March.

seasonal_wine.name: Mádi Bornapok (Mád Wine Days), late September
seasonal_wine.description: Mád, the spiritual heart of top-tier Tokaj viticulture, hosts the Mádi Bornapok festival in late September, just before harvest begins. The village's leading estates open their cellar doors simultaneously, and the event draws wine professionals and consumers from Budapest and abroad. New vintages are poured, and the village streets fill with producer stands. The festival coincides with the peak of the Furmint vine's pre-harvest ripening, making vineyard walks particularly vivid.
what_to_taste: Single-vineyard dry Furmint from Mád estates
seasonal_wine.tip: Book accommodation in Mád or Tarcal at least two months ahead for the festival weekend; the village has limited capacity and fills quickly for this annual event.

seasonal_wine.name: Aszú berry-picking season, early October
seasonal_wine.recurrence_pattern: annual, early to mid October, vintage-dependent
seasonal_wine.description: The most labour-intensive and extraordinary moment in the Tokaj wine calendar is the selective hand-picking of individual botrytised Aszú berries from the vineyard. Each grape is individually inspected and picked by hand when the botrytis fungus has shrivelled it to a raisin-like state of concentrated sugar. In a typical year this begins in early October after the dry wines have been harvested in September, though the timing shifts substantially with vintage conditions. Experiences are offered by Royal Tokaji, Disznókő and Oremus.
what_to_taste: Tokaji Aszú 5 puttonyos (current vintage if released)
seasonal_wine.tip: Aszú harvest experiences must be booked months in advance; contact estates directly in the spring before the October harvest. Disznókő, Royal Tokaji and Oremus all offer harvest participation programmes.

seasonal_wine.name: Tokaji Borfesztivál (Tokaj Wine Festival), late May
seasonal_wine.description: The Tokaj Wine Festival takes place in late May in the historic wine town of Tokaj at the confluence of the Tisza and Bodrog rivers. The event presents the region's producers to domestic and international visitors, with a strong focus on Aszú and Szamorodni as well as the growing dry Furmint category. The festival location in Tokaj town includes walks through the historic Rákóczi Cellar complex beneath the town centre.
seasonal_wine.tip: The Rákóczi Cellar beneath Tokaj town is open during the festival for a candlelit tasting that provides an atmospheric introduction to the region's heritage.

seasonal_wine.name: Szamorodni harvest season, September-October
seasonal_wine.description: Szamorodni, meaning 'as it grew' in Polish, is harvested as whole bunches without selective Aszú berry selection; whatever proportion of botrytised grapes happens to be on the cluster is included. The dry (száraz) style, oxidatively aged in part-filled casks, is Tokaj's answer to dry sherry in character if not production method. The sweet (édes) style is a luscious, amber-coloured wine of moderate residual sugar. Both styles are released in the spring following harvest. The Szamorodni season follows the regular dry Furmint harvest in early to mid September.
seasonal_wine.tip: Szamorodni száraz pairs strikingly well with the stuffed paprika and foie gras of the local gastronomy; ask tasting room staff to pair it at cellar door visits.

seasonal_wine.name: Aszú barrel release for trade, spring following harvest
seasonal_wine.recurrence_pattern: annual, spring 18-36 months after harvest vintage
seasonal_wine.description: Tokaji Aszú must spend a minimum of 18 months in oak casks followed by bottle ageing before release. The main trade and press tastings of newly released Aszú vintages occur in April and May, typically 18 to 36 months after the harvest, depending on the producer's style. Leading importers and buyers from London, New York and Tokyo converge on the Hegyalja and Budapest for these release tastings; it is the equivalent of Bordeaux en-primeur week for the Tokaj trade.
seasonal_wine.tip: The trade tasting schedule is published by the National Council of Wine Communities of Hungary; contact major producers directly to arrange barrel samples if attending as a buyer.

seasonal_wine.name: VinCE Budapest wine trade fair, January
seasonal_wine.description: VinCE Budapest is Hungary's largest annual wine trade fair, held at the SYMA Hall in Budapest in mid to late January. Tokaj producers take a significant presence at the fair, making it the first major annual platform to taste the Tokaj portfolio across all styles. Trade buyers, journalists, importers and consumers attend; the fair typically spans three days and includes masterclasses on Furmint, Aszú and the broader Hungarian wine landscape.
seasonal_wine.tip: The Tokaj section is usually in the central hall; arrive at opening on the trade day for the widest access to producers before the afternoon crowd arrives.

seasonal_wine.name: Summer cellar and heritage season, July-August
seasonal_wine.description: July and August bring the main tourist season to the Tokaj wine region, with visitors from Budapest and Central Europe exploring the cellar villages of Hercegkút, Tarcal and Tokaj town. The heat of the Hegyalja summer makes the cool, stone-carved underground cellars especially attractive for visits. Several producers run extended cellar walk-and-taste programmes at this time of year, and the Tokaj region's heritage cafes and restaurants are at full capacity. The dry Furmint harvest begins at the end of August in warmer vintages.
what_to_taste: Chilled dry Furmint as a summer aperitif
seasonal_wine.tip: The Gombos-hegyi cellar village in Hercegkút is the UNESCO-listed Swabian cellar street where visitors can walk between open cellars in the evening; arrive before 18:00 to avoid the tour-group overlap.

seasonal_wine.name: Dry wine harvest, September
seasonal_wine.recurrence_pattern: annual, early to mid September, vintage-dependent
seasonal_wine.description: The dry Furmint and Hárslevelü harvest begins in early to mid September, ahead of the Aszú berry selection. The grapes destined for Szamorodni are harvested next as whole clusters in mid September. The valley villages of Mád, Tarcal and Tolcsva are the most active at harvest, with mobile presses visible in the vineyard lanes. This is an authentic moment to visit Tokaj for those interested in the mechanics of winemaking rather than the finished-bottle experience.
what_to_taste: Freshly pressed Furmint grape must (at cellar doors)
Previous vintage dry Furmint compared to the new barrel sample
seasonal_wine.tip: Disznókő and Royal Tokaji both offer harvest visitor programmes where guests can participate in the early picking; book in July as places are limited.

seasonal_wine.name: Tarcal Borünnep (Tarcal Wine Festival), autumn
seasonal_wine.recurrence_pattern: annual, late September to mid October
seasonal_wine.description: Tarcal, the village immediately east of Tokaj town and home to Disznókő and other major estates, hosts the Tarcal Borünnep wine festival in late September or early October. Producers from the Tarcal and Tokaj-adjacent terroir open their cellar doors and pour across all styles, with a particular emphasis on the Lapis and Mézes Mály vineyard sites that surround the village. Local gastronomy stalls offer the traditional Zemplén food pairings of stuffed cabbage, lángos and catfish dishes.
seasonal_wine.tip: Tarcal makes a better base for festival weekend than Tokaj town itself, as the village is quieter and accommodation is closer to the major cellar doors.

seasonal_wine.name: Tokaji Borvidék Ünnepe (Tokaj Wine Region Day), autumn
seasonal_wine.description: The Tokaj Wine Region Day is an annual celebration coordinated across the 27 villages of the Tokaj Hegyalja PDO, recognising the collective heritage and contemporary achievement of the wine region. Events include open cellar days in multiple villages simultaneously, heritage walks through the UNESCO-listed landscape, and joint tastings at the regional wine house in Tokaj town. The day is anchored to the harvest season and celebrates both the viticultural year and the living landscape of the Hegyalja.
seasonal_wine.tip: Coordinating village stops on the Region Day is well served by hiring a driver from Budapest or renting a bicycle from Tokaj town; distances between the key cellar villages are 5-15 km on flat valley roads.
